source.title: AI in Parish Life: What Every Catholic Needs to Know | Catholic Futurist

summary.title_hr: AI u župnom životu: Što svaki katolik treba znati | Katolički futurist
summary.abstract_hr: Ova epizoda podcasta "Catholic Futurist" istražuje ulogu umjetne inteligencije u katoličkom župnom životu i evangelizaciji. Gosti Edmundo Reyes i Edmund Mitchell, zajedno s voditeljem Benjaminom Crockettom, raspravljaju o tome kako AI može preuzeti administrativne zadatke ("misijska potpora"), oslobađajući svećenike za izravnu pastoralnu skrb ("izravna misija"). Naglašavaju potrebu za mudrim i razboritim korištenjem AI-a, stavljajući ljudsko dostojanstvo u središte, te ističu kako Crkva može učiti iz načina na koji se ljudi otvaraju chatbotovima. Razgovor nudi praktične primjere primjene AI-a u katekizmu i propovijedanju, te poziva na proaktivno uključivanje Crkve u digitalnu revoluciju.
key_points: Papa Franjo smatra AI uzbudljivom, ali i zastrašujućom tehnologijom, naglašavajući da se strah od nje često temelji na neznanju.
Crkva je prešla od koncepta "digitalnog kontinenta" (mjesta za evangelizaciju) do "digitalne kulture" (uronjenosti u digitalno kao dio svakodnevnog života), što zahtijeva prilagodbu pristupa.
AI može značajno pomoći u "misijskoj potpori" (financije, HR, tehnologija) u župama i dijecezama, oslobađajući resurse za "izravnu misiju" (evangelizacija, sakramenti, pastoralna skrb).
Važno je AI shvatiti kao "partnera u razmišljanju", a ne samo kao alat, što podrazumijeva promjenu svjetonazora prema složenijem i sinodalnom pristupu u službi.
Činjenica da se ljudi otvaraju chatbotovima više nego ljudima ukazuje na potrebu da Crkva razvije dublje slušanje i empatiju u pastoralnoj skrbi.
AI može poboljšati pripremu katekizma i propovijedi, ali nikada ne smije zamijeniti ljudsku molitvu, razboritost i osobni susret u prenošenju vjere.
Buduće župe trebale bi se usredotočiti na edukaciju o medijskoj i AI pismenosti, koristiti AI za smanjenje troškova medijske produkcije i analizu podataka o župnim teritorijima.
summary.title_en: AI in parish life: What every Catholic needs to know | Katolički futurist
summary.abstract_en: This episode of the podcast "Catholic Futurist" explores the role of artificial intelligence in Catholic parish life and evangelization. Guests Edmundo Reyes and Edmund Mitchell, together with host Benjamin Crockett, discuss how AI can take over administrative tasks ("mission support"), freeing priests for direct pastoral care ("direct mission"). They emphasize the need for wise and prudent use of AI, putting human dignity at the center, and point out how the Church can learn from the way people open up to chatbots. The conversation offers practical examples of AI application in catechism and preaching, and calls for the proactive involvement of the Church in the digital revolution.
key_topics_en: AI as a tool and partner
The role of AI in parish life
the future of parishes with AI
key_points_en: Pope Francis considers AI an exciting but also terrifying technology, emphasizing that the fear of it is often based on ignorance.

The Church has moved from the concept of the "digital continent" (a place for evangelization) to "digital culture" (immersion in the digital as a part of everyday life), which requires an adaptation of the approach.
AI can significantly help in "missionary support" (finances, HR, technology) in parishes and dioceses, freeing up resources for "direct mission" (evangelization, sacraments, pastoral care).
It is important to understand AI as a "partner in thinking", and not only as a tool, which implies a change of worldview towards a more complex and synodal approach in ministry.
The fact that people open up to chatbots more than to people indicates the need for the Church to develop deeper listening and empathy in pastoral care.
AI can improve the preparation of catechism and sermons, but it must never replace human prayer, prudence, and personal encounter in the transmission of faith.
Future parishes should focus on education about media and AI literacy, use AI for reducing media production costs and data analysis about parish territories.
